column2: An organization wants to require screen locks, configure corporate email, and restrict unauthorized apps on employee mobile devices. What type of system should it use?
column5:
Why

MDM centrally applies mobile-device policies, including access controls, app restrictions, and corporate email configuration.

Real Help Desk

When an enterprise phone “sets itself up,” MDM may be applying policy rather than a user manually configuring each setting.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.5; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3

column2: A phone is about to synchronize a large amount of business data, and the user has a limited cellular plan. What should you verify before allowing the sync over mobile data?
column4: Verify data restrictions, data caps, and transfer costs, and prefer Wi-Fi when appropriate.
column5:
Why

Synchronization can move enough data to create cost or cap problems. The approved source specifically warns that cellular/mobile and 802.11 choices can have financial consequences.

Real Help Desk

For managed devices, policy may disable cellular downloads or force certain network choices.
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.5; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v2.0, Objective 1.3
column10: A+::220-1201::1.3 A+::220-1201::Domain1-MobileDevices A+::220-1201::MobileDeviceNetworks Basic HighYield Scenario Synchronization Cellular Source::Messer-v170

column2: A user adds a business cloud-storage app to a phone. What two setup tasks are commonly required before data appears on the device?
column4: Authenticate to the service and select which items to synchronize.
column5:
Why

Business applications commonly require username/password authentication and synchronization choices before data appears on the mobile device.

Exam Trap

Installing the app is not always the same as syncing data. Account setup and sync selection may still be required.
